title: oenothera biennis subsp. grandiflora
intro: Oenothera biennis subsp. grandiflora, commonly known as evening primrose, is a flowering plant in the family Onagraceae, characterized by its tall stature and showy, fragrant flowers. The plant contains linoleic acid and gamma-linolenic acid, which have been used in traditional medicine for their anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The medicinal properties of Oenothera biennis subsp. grandiflora have been used to alleviate symptoms of menopause, such as hot flashes and night sweats, as well as to soothe skin conditions like eczema and acne. In herbal preparations, the plant's leaves and flowers are often dried and used to make teas, salves, and tinctures that are used to support overall health and well-being.
